pattern: Water above each bar is bounded by the shorter of the tallest walls to its left and right, which two converging pointers can track in one pass.
hints: For a single position, the water it holds depends only on the highest bars on either side of it.
Precomputing the running maximum from the left and from the right turns each position into a simple min-minus-height calculation.
Walk a left and right pointer inward, always advancing the side with the smaller running max; the side you advance is guaranteed bounded, so add maxSoFar minus its height.

edge_inputs.description: classic uneven skyline holding water in several dips
deep central basin between tall walls
two equal walls with a flat floor between them
zeros at both ends cannot hold water

stress.description: random skyline over a moderate height range to exercise many fill/spill transitions
stress.note: moderate range produces frequent local minima that trap water
